flashcards.back: A BST constructed to minimize the expected search cost given known access probabilities (p_i for successful searches of key k_i, q_i for unsuccessful searches between keys). Built using dynamic programming.
c[i][j] = min over r from i+1 to j of {c[i][r-1] + c[r][j]} + w[i][j], where w[i][j] = sum of probabilities p_(i+1)...p_j and q_i...q_j. Base case: c[i][i] = q_i.
w[i][j] = sum of all access probabilities for keys and dummy keys in the range i to j. It represents the total probability mass of the subtree and is added at each level when a root is chosen.
Stores the index of the optimal root for the subtree containing keys k_(i+1) to k_j. Used to reconstruct the actual optimal BST structure after the DP computation is complete.
Reduces the standard O(n^3) DP to O(n^2) by exploiting the monotonicity property: r[i][j-1] <= r[i][j] <= r[i+1][j]. This limits the range of root candidates to check for each subproblem.
